key_insight: Applying Six Sigma DMAIC to automotive resistance spot welding eliminated spot crack defects by addressing operator, equipment, inspection, and positioning causes.
engineering_relevance: The study shows that structured quality-control workflows can quickly improve weld reliability and reduce manufacturing scrap in automotive assembly lines.
method: Defined the defect problem in Rear Door spot welding
Measured baseline performance with DPMO and sigma capability
Analyzed root causes using Pareto and Fishbone tools
Improved the process with retraining, equipment replacement, added inspection points, and a gun guide fixture
evidence_signal: Baseline performance was 20 defects in 2,400 units (DPMO 2,083.33; 4.37 sigma), and after corrective actions 2,380 units were produced with zero spot crack defects (DPMO 0; above 6 sigma).
watch_item: Results come from a single case study, so sustained gains may depend on consistent maintenance, operator adherence, and replication across other weld lines.
paper_title: Implementation of Six Sigma DMAIC Methodology for Eliminating Spot Crack Defects in Automotive Resistance Spot Welding Process
